conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm inquiring about. My son Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'm happy to help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Before I can determine your eligibility, I need to read the official guidance on Child Benefit rules. Let me do that now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have the information I need to assess your eligibility for Alex.

To determine if you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Living abroad can affect eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

**Which country are you currently living in?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You may still be able to get Child Benefit while living in Spain in certain circumstances.

**Are you or your partner a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad), or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant and I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my decision:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the official UK government guidance, the main requirement for Child Benefit is that you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and **you live in the UK**.

While Alex is 13 years old and meets the age requirement, you are currently living in Spain and do not meet any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit from abroad. The exceptions that could allow someone living abroad to claim are:

- Being a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad)
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain conditions met

Since you've confirmed that none of these apply to your situation, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you would be able to make a claim at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education/training) and you live in the UK. While Alex is 13 years old and within the age requirement, the claimant does not live in the UK - they are living in Spain. The rules state that you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. However, the claimant has confirmed they are not a Crown servant and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, which are the main routes to claiming Child Benefit while living abroad. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 13), and is inquiring about Child Benefit. While Alex meets the age requirement (under 16), the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit from abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme). The primary eligibility requirement is that you live in the UK, which the claimant does not satisfy.
